claim_text: Adamson et al. used this approach to train a DL model for image reconstruction with a feature distance loss measured against an SSFD pre-trained model encoder.

sub_claims.text: desai2021 used SSFD-encoder-based feature distance as a training loss for DL MR reconstruction.

evidence.snippet: A pixel-wise l1 supervised loss L_sup is computed for supervised examples y(s). Undersampled examples y(u) are passed through the Augmentation Pipeline (see §4.1).
A pixel-wise l1 consistency loss L_cons is computed between the model reconstruction outputs of input undersampled examples with and without augmentation (Eq. 1).
All methods used a 2D U-Net network with a complex-l1 training objective for both supervised and consistency losses.

evidence.section: E.1 Latent Space vs Pixel-level Consistency
evidence.snippet: We compare enforcing consistency at the pixel-level output image versus learned latent representations at varying U-Net resolution levels. Let R_k be k-th resolution level at which consistency is enforced, where k in {1,2,3,4} since our U-Net architecture had 4 pooling layers. For the k-th resolution level, we enforce consistency after the final convolution in the encoder, and after the transpose convolution in the decoder.

evidence.section: E.1 Latent Space vs Pixel-level Consistency
evidence.snippet: For in-distribution data, latent space consistency at any resolution level performed on par with pixel-level consistency. However, for OOD data, it performed considerably worse than pixel-level consistency ... we find that pixel-level consistency was a better technique for capturing the semantics of global distribution shifts such as motion for accelerated MRI reconstruction.

sub_claims.paper_value: pixel-wise L1 (complex-L1) supervised + pixel-wise L1 consistency loss; an ablation explores latent-space consistency at the in-model U-Net encoder/decoder resolution levels, not a separate pretrained SSFD encoder
sub_claims.claim_value: feature distance loss measured against an SSFD pre-trained model encoder
sub_claims.nuance: VORTEX (desai2021) uses pixel-level L1 losses and, in an ablation, consistency in the reconstruction U-Net's own latent layers (Section E.1). It does not use SSFD, nor a pretrained self-supervised feature encoder, nor a perceptual/feature-distance loss against any fixed encoder. The ablation actually finds pixel-level consistency outperforms latent-space consistency under OOD perturbations.

remediation.suggested_edit: Remove the desai2021 (VORTEX) citation from this sentence. VORTEX trains with pixel-wise L1 supervised and pixel-wise L1 consistency losses (with an ablation over U-Net latent-level consistency using the reconstruction network's own encoder), and never uses SSFD or any feature distance measured against a pretrained (SSFD) encoder. Author/paper note: the sentence attributes the work to 'Adamson et al.' but cites a Desai-first-author paper; even the SSFD paper itself (adamson2021) in the current bibliography does not train a reconstruction DL model with SSFD as a feature-distance loss — it defines SSFD as a reconstruction *quality metric* (its U-Net is trained self-supervised with l2 on an in-painting pretext task). If the manuscript intends to cite work that adopted SSFD as a reconstruction training loss, a different primary source is needed; if no such primary exists, consider rescoping the claim or removing it.

phrasings_tried: SSFD (self-supervised feature distance) used as training loss in desai2021
feature distance loss / perceptual loss against a pretrained encoder in VORTEX
pretrained SSFD encoder used to compute feature distance in MR reconstruction training
latent-space / feature-space consistency loss in VORTEX and whether it invokes a fixed pretrained encoder
adjudicator cross-check: does any Adamson/Desai paper in the bib (adamson2021, desai2022) use SSFD as a training loss for DL reconstruction?

section_checklist.section: E.1 Latent Space vs Pixel-level Consistency
B Extended Related Work (VORTEX vs MRAugment / Noise2Recon)

attestation.closest_adjacent: Appendix E.1 'Latent Space vs Pixel-level Consistency' (content.txt lines 66-67): VORTEX ablates enforcing the L1 consistency loss at learned latent representations inside its own U-Net (after the encoder's final conv, at the bottleneck, or after the decoder's transpose conv) rather than at the pixel output. This is the closest concept to a 'feature distance loss' in the paper, but the features come from the reconstruction model's own U-Net encoder trained end-to-end, not from a pretrained SSFD encoder, and the paper finds pixel-level consistency works better for OOD settings. No SSFD, no self-supervised pretrained encoder, and no perceptual/feature-distance term is used as a training loss anywhere in desai2021.
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: desai2021 does not attribute an SSFD feature-distance loss to any other primary either; SSFD is simply not discussed. Adamson et al. is cited in desai2021 for Noise2Recon (consistency denoising, balanced sampling), not for an SSFD-based loss. Adjudicator also cross-checked adamson2021 (the SSFD paper) and desai2022 (SKM-TEA): adamson2021 positions SSFD as a quality metric and trains its pretext U-Net with l2; desai2022 does not mention SSFD. No bibliography paper supports the sub-claim, so MISATTRIBUTED is not warranted.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript's claim describes adopting SSFD-based feature-distance as a training loss. The nearest passage in desai2021 (latent-space consistency ablation) is a different construct (internal U-Net feature consistency, L1, no pretrained SSFD encoder, and reported as inferior to pixel-level consistency for OOD). Citing desai2021 here is out of context relative to an SSFD-loss adoption claim.
